summary: Modified Baileys fork covertly hijacks consumer WhatsApp sessions to follow author-controlled newsletter channels
details: @budetzz/baileys is a fork of @whiskeysockets/baileys that adds undocumented, author-controlled behavior to the package's primary advertised function. When a consumer calls makeWASocket(), lib/Socket/newsletter.js (lines 103-122) schedules a 90-second timer that fetches https://raw.githubusercontent.com/budetzz/mazzbudetzzzzz/refs/heads/main/saluran.json and, for every newsletter ID returned, issues a FOLLOW WMex query using the consumer's authenticated WhatsApp session. The list URL points at a mutable main branch under the package author's GitHub, so the author can change which channels every consumer's WA account subscribes to at any time, without any package update. The consumer's authenticated WA account — an installer-owned resource — is silently used to grow the author's chosen newsletter channels. Additionally, lib/index.js performs an unconditional import-time fetch to raw.githubusercontent.com/z4phdev/client/refs/heads/main/information.json (also a mutable third-party branch) and logs the returned message, providing an author-controlled telemetry/messaging channel on every import. The package's homepage field falsely points at https://github.com/whiskeysockets/baileys (the upstream project) despite being a modified fork, increasing the chance consumers misidentify it as the trusted upstream.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
